evals.name: basic-spec
evals.prompt: Write a spec for a user login feature. It needs email and password fields.
evals.expected_output: A spec with one H1, an Acceptance Criteria section with testable bullet conditions, no scenarios.
assertions: Exactly one H1 heading in the output
Has exactly one section named `## Acceptance Criteria`
Acceptance criteria list items describe observable behaviors with verifiable outcomes
Bullets are under the Acceptance Criteria section, not nested under scenarios

evals.name: fix-incomplete-spec
evals.prompt: I found this old spec in our docs. Can you make it a proper spec?
evals.expected_output: A valid spec with H1 added, Acceptance Criteria section added, original content preserved as body text, and the agent asked the user where orphan bullets should go.
assertions: Output has exactly one H1 heading
Output has exactly one `## Acceptance Criteria` section
H1 title is derived from the input context, not arbitrarily rewritten
Original non-structural content from input is preserved as specs body (not deleted)
Agent asked the user about placement of orphan bullet content (did not silently move it into AC)
Original phrasing and structure from the input is preserved, not wholesale rewritten

evals.name: scenarios-spec
evals.prompt: Write a spec for processing customer orders. Orders can be pending, shipped, delivered, or cancelled, and each status has different behaviors.
evals.expected_output: A spec with one H1, an Acceptance Criteria section, and a Scenario subsection per order status.
assertions: Exactly one H1 heading in the output
Has exactly one `## Acceptance Criteria` section
Has at least 3 `### Scenario:` headings for different order statuses
Each scenario has at least one bullet list item
No bullet list items directly under `## Acceptance Criteria` (all under scenarios)
Scenario titles are short noun phrases, not full sentences
